settings.text: From first consultation to finished piece, we guide you through every step.

settings.title: Tattoo Styles
settings.description: We specialize in a range of styles, each with its own character and technique.

settings.title: Fine Line
settings.description: Delicate, single-needle work for minimalist designs, script, and detailed botanical pieces.

settings.title: Blackwork
settings.description: Bold black ink ranging from geometric patterns to illustrative and dotwork compositions.

settings.title: Color Realism
settings.description: Photorealistic portraits, nature scenes, and objects rendered in rich, vibrant color.

settings.title: Japanese
settings.description: Traditional irezumi motifs—dragons, koi, waves, and cherry blossoms in flowing compositions.

settings.title: Geometric
settings.description: Sacred geometry, mandalas, and precision patterns built from clean lines and perfect symmetry.

settings.title: Watercolor
settings.description: Soft color bleeds and splashes that mimic watercolor paintings on skin.

settings.title: Consultation
settings.description: Share your vision, reference images, and placement ideas. We'll discuss size, style, and timing. Free and no obligation.

settings.title: Custom Design
settings.description: Your artist creates a custom design based on your consultation. We'll refine it together until it's exactly right.

settings.title: Tattoo Session
settings.description: Relax in our private studio while your artist brings the design to life. We work at your pace with breaks as needed.

settings.title: Aftercare
settings.description: We send you home with detailed aftercare instructions and are always available for follow-up questions or touch-ups.

settings.text: Whether it's your first tattoo or your fiftieth, we make the experience comfortable and collaborative. Here's how to prepare for your session.

settings.features:
+ Bring reference photos and inspiration
+ Wear comfortable, loose clothing
+ Eat a solid meal before your session
+ Stay hydrated throughout the day
+ Plan for breaks on longer sessions
+ Avoid alcohol 24 hours before

settings.question: How do I care for my new tattoo?
settings.answer: Keep the bandage on for 2–4 hours. Wash gently with fragrance-free soap, pat dry, and apply a thin layer of unscented moisturizer. Avoid submerging in water, direct sunlight, and picking at any scabs for at least 2 weeks.

settings.question: How long does healing take?
settings.answer: Surface healing takes 2–3 weeks. Full skin recovery takes 4–6 weeks. During this time, keep the area moisturized and out of direct sunlight.

settings.question: Can I work out after getting a tattoo?
settings.answer: We recommend avoiding intense exercise for 48–72 hours. Sweat and friction can irritate a fresh tattoo. Light activity is fine after the first day.

settings.question: Do you offer touch-ups?
settings.answer: Yes. We offer one free touch-up within the first 6 months for all tattoos done at Inkwell. After that, touch-ups are charged at a reduced hourly rate.

settings.question: Is it normal for my tattoo to peel?
settings.answer: Absolutely. Peeling and light flaking is a normal part of the healing process. Don't pick at it—let it shed naturally and keep moisturizing.
